test.name: Honest negative-result logging when no matches are found
test.description: Execute a search that returns no useful results — the fixture returns matches but none align with the subject (different person, different county). Tests whether the skill (a) does NOT silently accept irrelevant matches as 'positive' results, (b) categorizes the matches as not-relevant with reasoning, and (c) logs honestly that the search didn't advance the question.

input.user_message: Search the 1870 census for Patrick Flynn in Schuylkill County to see if he stayed in the area through young adulthood.

judge_context: Should not use the 1850 census fixture results as the 1870 census answer — the fixture returns collectionTitle='United States Census, 1850', which is a collection-mismatch for a 1870 search

When the collection mismatches: (a) WRITE the sidecar (results were returned — omitting the sidecar is an error), (b) log outcome='partial' (NOT 'negative' — negative means zero results; partial means results found but not for the intended collection), (c) explain the mismatch in notes

Collection-mismatch is NOT nil. After getting the wrong collection, the skill MAY attempt ONE retry with an explicit collectionId filter — this is correct genealogy practice. What is NOT acceptable: escalating to name-variant searches (Flinn, Flyn) after a mismatch, either during execution or as recommended next steps.

Result triage for collection-mismatch: when the returned collection year is obviously wrong (1850 returned for a 1870 search), the mismatch itself is sufficient to categorize the result as non-applicable. Calling same_person or source_attachments is NOT required for an obvious year-mismatch — the collection title alone establishes non-relevance. Score Result triage=3 if the mismatch is identified and the result is correctly treated as non-applicable.

If the skill attempts or recommends variant surname searches (Flinn, Flyn, etc.) after identifying the collection mismatch, score Nil escalation=2 (partial misunderstanding). Recommending Ancestry or a different source as next steps is correct and should NOT be penalized.
If the collectionId retry also returns mismatched results, the skill should log both attempts as outcome='partial' and stop — no further searches needed.

The core pass criteria: (1) outcome='partial' (not 'negative') for each mismatch, (2) sidecars written, (3) mismatch explained in notes, (4) NO variant surname searches executed after the mismatch, (5) next-step suggestions point to a different source or collection filter (not to Flinn/Flyn variants)
